articleSummary: This instructional guide addresses a common Linux performance warning by providing a ready-to-run shell script that disables transparent huge pages (THP) for MongoDB on Debian and Ubuntu systems. The core thesis is that disabling THP is a mandatory configuration step to prevent memory fragmentation and latency spikes, directly supporting MongoDB’s official performance recommendations. Targeted at system administrators and DevOps engineers, the article delivers a quick, automated fix while referencing the official MongoDB documentation for deeper technical context. The tone is strictly practical and tutorial-oriented, with no figurative language or framing devices, focusing exclusively on actionable commands and kernel-level system adjustments.
